Bundle: A package of assets or content grouped together for loading or saving in the application
Multiple: Refers to multiple audio or motion assets being selected or loaded
With Camera: Assets or sequences that include associated camera movements or settings
Folder Actions: Actions performed on a folder, such as organizing or loading contents
External: Content or files coming from outside the main system or repository
Service: An AI backend service that generates responses
Player: The user interacting with the AI/chat system
Ollama: A specific AI service provider or platform
Dummy Read Speed: The speed used for a placeholder or simulated reading
Template: Predefined formatting or structure for chat messages
Entry: A message or log item in a list
Regenerate: To create again or refresh content previously generated
Replay: To play again a recorded audio or video
Remove Above: To delete all entries positioned before the current one
Remove Below: To delete all entries positioned after the current one
Unknown: Data or value that could not be determined
XRay: A visual effect making the model partially transparent to see underlying parts.
Guitar Mode: A mode related to guitar playing animation or control.
Pole: A physical object like a pole accessory.
Motion: A predefined animation sequence for accessory movement
Cache: A saved copy of 3D models or motions
Actor Playlist: A list or sequence of actors to be loaded or played
Pro: A version or license tier of the software
Setting: A configuration or parameter for morphs or textures
Retainable: Morphs or textures that remain after reset
Reload: Reload the actor or model to refresh its data from the source
Global: Applies to all actors or system-wide settings
Ignore rotation of attach bone: Do not apply the rotation of the attach bone to the attached object
Occlusion: In AR, the visual blocking or hiding of virtual objects by real-world objects
Hide Ground: Hide the ground plane detected by AR system
Chorus: A repeated section in music where multiple voices or instruments sing/perform in unison
Left Trigger: The left trigger button on a game controller
Input Source: The origin or device providing input data
Swing By Time: A method to modulate output over a time interval with a swinging motion
Paint Side: Side of the model to paint on, e.g., front or back
Glow: A visual shader effect enhancing brightness on painted parts
Base: The primary layer or foundational color in painting
Slope: Rate of change of height on the mesh
String Connection: Horizontal connections between vertices in the string mode
Gradual Enable: A feature to slowly activate cloth simulation over time
Export Shape: An option to export the collider shape data
Visual & Colliders: Visual appearance and physical colliders for interaction in a 3D environment
Breast From: Starting point or reference for breast shape or position adjustment
Breast To: Ending point or target for breast shape or position adjustment
Density: Amount of particles or visual elements within the pattern
Curve: A graphical curve used to modify dissolve progression
Jito: Could refer to a specific facial expression, a user-defined blendshape, or a technical term requiring clarification.
Sole Height: Height of the foot sole relative to the ground
Fluid: General term for liquid being simulated
Special Shader Mode: A unique rendering mode that applies specific visual effects
GlowColorData: Data representing color settings for a glow effect
Glow w/ Music: Glow effect synchronized with music rhythm or beats
Video: A texture sourced from a video file
Use Accessory Position: Whether to consider position of an attached accessory when calculating the pose
Propagate: Apply changes to child bones or joints
InfluenceBone: A bone that influences other bones when being animated
Custom Inherit Motion: A setting that defines how motion is inherited from a parent bone with custom rules
Relative To Floor: Position or rotation measured with the floor as the reference plane
Max Twist: Maximum rotational angle allowed on the leg or foot
Open: Indicating the leg or foot is in an open position
Mode: Could refer to different operational modes for Masking, wrapping, or cutting off. Clarification needed if possible.
Custom 1: User-defined material category specified by a plugin or user
Custom 2: User-defined material category specified by a plugin or user
Options: A menu or set of configurable parameters related to material categories
Lips: A predefined material group representing lip textures or colors.
Floor: Refers to the ground surface in the scene
Surface: The outer layer or material finish of a 3D model
Visulize: A debug visualization that display the colliders
Depth: The level of detail in collision detection
Mesh: A 3D model surface made of vertices, edges, and faces
Geometry Colliders: Primitive colliders such as box, sphere, or capsule shapes
Exclusion: A setting to exclude certain meshes or layers from collision detection
Override Depth: A manual setting to replace the automatic depth value in collision detection
Acceleration Mode: A mode that controls how forces or velocities are applied
Bonciness: Typo for 'Bounciness': The property defining how much an object bounces
Lock 0 Limit: Lock the joint if limit is set to 0
Target Actor: The character model in the scene that is used as reference
Dressing System: A system managing character clothing and appearance
Custom Items: User-defined or user-imported items not included in default sets
Transition: The process of changing the appearance of items or materials
Dissolve: A visual effect where the outfit fades or disappears gradually
Static Inclusive / Static Exclusive / Dy: Settings related to collision groups or layers defining which objects are considered as static or dynamic and whether collisions between these groups are included or excluded.
Feet: The part of the body of a person or animal below the ankle on which they stand or walk
Sweat Collision: The interaction between sweat droplets and another surface
Water Interaction: Any interaction between objects and water
Metallic: A shader property that gives a metallic appearance in graphics
Entitlement: License or permission to use certain software features or content
System: A separate in-app settings or configuration menu
Stocking: A part or feature of a shape, possibly referring to a stock or standard section
Inside Bump: A raised feature or protrusion on the inside part of the shape
Outside Bump: A raised feature or protrusion on the outside part of the shape
Bump Effect: A visual or physical effect causing the surface to appear raised or textured
Table Size: Size of an internal lookup table used for simulation computations
Alternate Group Size: Grouping size of particles or elements processed alternately during simulation
Always Rebuild: Flag to always rebuild the simulation mesh or data structures each frame
Reverse Even Substeps: An option to reverse the order of even-numbered substeps in simulation to improve accuracy
Dynamic: Simulation mode where cloth moves and reacts to forces freely
Motion Curve: A curve defining the speed or progression of an animation over time
Delay: A lag or wait time inserted within motion or animation
Sex Overlay: Apply procedural motion on top of the current motion and pose
Color Mode: The mode or method used to display or process colors (e.g., RGB, HSV)
Clip: A setting related to alpha clipping.
Nipple: Anatomical part of a character model
Tattoo: A texture representing tattoo patterns applied on character skin
Spot: A type of light effect texture
Extent: Range or scope of movement in dancing
Use Loudness For: Using loudness to control intensity of dance moves
Pussy Up / Down: Vertical movement or positioning of the female genital area
Pussy Front / Back: Forward/backward movement or positioning of the female genital area
Anchoring: Fixing or stabilizing a part of the body during motion
Lower: Move the character to a lower position
Role: A character's function or job within the motion system
Feet Distance: The distance between a character's feet to measure stance or step width
Lean: The angle or tilt of a character's upper body
Pairing: The act of linking two characters for interaction
Heel: The heel part of the foot
[Zero]: Sets the character model in standard pose
Travel distance: The distance an object moves during an animation or interaction
Allocation of movement between male and: Distribution of movement types or amounts for male and female characters
Concert Mode: A camera mode that stays at a fixed position at distance
Shift: A camera movement or position shift
Near: Camera focus or zoom set to a near distance
Stablizer: Stabilizer - reduces camera jitter or shake
Freefly Cam: A camera mode allowing free movement in all directions
Follow Actor: An instruction to track or follow another character in motion.
Min Distance: Minimum distance a character should keep from others
Unknown Bones: bones not recognized by the system
Output Buffer: A reserved area in memory where recorded data is temporarily stored before saving or processing
Beat Cycle: A rhythmic interval used for timing camera movements to music or events
Target: An object or character that the camera focuses on
Tracking Mode: Camera movement that follows the target position
Center / Stage Center: The center point of the stage or environment
Bend Penis: An animation parameter controlling penile bending for realistic motion
Projector Scene Example: An example scene using a projector to display video
LED Screen Example: An example scene using an LED screen to display video
Dangling objects: Accessories or items hanging loosely from the character model
Physics Joint: A connection between two physical bodies enabling constrained movement
Reduction Rate: A multiplier to reduce applied forces or speeds
Firmness A: A coefficient defining joint stiffness on one side of the range
Firmness B: A coefficient defining joint stiffness on the opposite side compared to Firmness A
Along Direction: Refers to a vector direction along which a force or constraint applies
Compliance: How much the particles respects the constraints
Reducing mass at each level: Decreasing the mass of components progressively through a hierarchy to improve simulation performance
Reduction Ratio: The ratio related to mass reduction per hierarchy level
Softbody: A deformable 3D object simulated to behave like soft materials (e.g., cloth, jelly)
Background Vibration: Subtle movement effect applied to the background to simulate vibration
Foreground Vibration: Subtle movement effect applied to the foreground elements to simulate vibration
Allocation: The process of determining which character does the spotlight point to
Adaptation: Adjustment process of exposure according to lighting changes
Stage: A physical or virtual performance area
Output: The final rendered image or buffer produced by the process
LED Mode: A display mode where the surface mimics an LED screen appearance
Wrap Mode: How the texture is applied when UV coordinates exceed the standard range (e.g. repeat, clamp, mirror)
Fit Texture: An option to automatically scale the texture to fit the geometry
Audio Visualizer: An embedded tool to visualize audio within the software
Blank: A placeholder or default empty texture
Recursive: Refers specifically to recursive ray tracing where rays are traced multiple times for multiple reflections.
Max Depth: The maximum number of recursion levels allowed for ray tracing.
Ray Length: The maximum distance a ray can travel in ray tracing before it is terminated.
Tools: Various utilities provided to adjust formation settings
Probe: A sampled point in the environment used for reflection or lighting calculations
Actor Floor: The floor area designated for characters or actors to stand on within the scene
Paper White: A configuration value that determines the brightness of white in HDR mode
Style: General visual appearance or theme of the light effect
Crystal: An object with a clear, glass-like appearance in the light effect
Ball: A generic spherical object possibly unrelated to light
Intensity: Brightness or strength of a visual effect
Camera Target: The point or object in the scene that the camera is focused on.
Prime Motion: The main motion assigned to the actor
Passthrough: A mode to display the real-world environment through device cameras
Middle Color: An intermediate color between sky and ground for gradient effect
Window: A literal window in a virtual environment
Flat: A flat shading style with no lighting effects
Prepass: An initial rendering pass done before the main rendering to prepare certain data
Adjustment: General color or image adjustment settings
Glow Box: A lighting effect that simulates glowing boxes within a scene
Gloss Circle: A circular highlight effect representing glossiness
Room Stage: A virtual or physical stage setup within a room environment
Edge Steps: A parameter defining levels or increments on edges
Frame: A single image in a sequence
Camera Model: A 3D model representing a physical camera device
Screen 1 / Screen 2: Specific screen instances or layers within the scene
Indoor (Sky Mode): A sky mode intended for indoor environment lighting
Stage / Pool: A performance stage or a pool area next to it
Runway: A narrow platform extended from the stage used by performers
Overlap Objects: Objects that physically intersect or cover each other in 3D space
Transition Effect: A visual effect applied when switching between scenes or images
Transition Mode: The method or style in which the transition effect is applied
V Shape: A type of edge shape used in the animation or effect
Accessory: A virtual item or attachment worn on the hand in VR (e.g., rings, bracelets)
River: A flowing body of water within the scene/environment
Water System: A complete simulation and rendering system for water effects
Pool: A static body of water, typically small and enclosed, like a swimming pool
Microphone: Software toggle for enabling/disabling microphone input
Custom Axis: Additional configurable axes for input devices
Secondary Action: An alternative or additional action triggered by the UI
Trackpad Mode: A mode that simulates joystick input using a trackpad
Game Controller / Gamepad: A physical input device such as an Xbox or PlayStation controller
Left Btn 1 / Left Btn 2 / Right Btn 1 /: Buttons located on the left or right side of the controller (specific buttons, e.g., shoulder buttons or face buttons)
Auto Tag: Automatically generated tags based on item properties
VR 120 3D: Virtual Reality video with 120-degree field of view in 3D
Pointer State: The status or mode of the mouse cursor (e.g., select, drag, hover)
Interaction: User input or engagement with the software interface
Slither: A motion characteristic describing smooth, snake-like movement.
room: A virtual chatroom or online space for text communication
Original: The initial or default texture/model before any changes
Blend: To combine multiple colors or textures
Label: Text shown on a UI element
stage ring: A physical ring of lights arranged on the stage
Edge: The boundary or outline of the material surface
Anchor: A reference point for transformations or physics calculations
Primitive Object: A basic geometric 3D shape used in modeling, such as cube, sphere, or cylinder
Stationary: An object that does not move or is fixed in place
Shadow Only: An object that is invisible but only casts a shadow
Pussy: Refers to a part of the female anatomy, used in a biological or anatomical context
Library: A collection of saved motions or assets
Sync: Synchronization of motion with audio or other events
Motion Shift: An adjustment or offset applied to the motion's position or timing
Cowgirl Sex: A sexual position with the female on top facing the partner
Contact: Physical touching between body parts or objects in the motion
Compression: Reducing the size or intensity of motion data
Sex From Behind: Refers to a sexual position viewed from behind
Drag: The force opposing an object's motion through a fluid (e.g., air resistance).
Loading: The process of loading data or resources into memory
Environment: The overall setting or backdrop of a scene (e.g., landscape, background objects)
Hidden: An object or element that is not visible in the user interface
Dance: A type of animation or motion sequence performed by a character or model
Filtering: The process of applying a digital filter to data
Feature Page: A specific page highlighting a particular feature in the software
Sit Back & Enjoy: A prompt to relax and watch content without interaction
Audio / Motion: Audio refers to sound files; Motion refers to animation data
